content.cn_text: 你将听到一位讲师讨论袋鼠肉的销售和消费。
content.en_text: You will hear a lecturer discussing the marketing and consumption of kangaroo meat.

content.cn_text: 当人们想到澳大利亚时。
content.en_text: When one thinks of Australia.

content.cn_text: 首先映入脑海的是袋鼠。
content.en_text: one of the first images that comes to mind is of the kangaroo.

content.cn_text: 小袋鼠是一种可爱的、不寻常的本土有袋动物，小袋鼠被称为“小袋鼠”，安全地放在妈妈的育儿袋里，这是澳大利亚明信片和海报上经常出现的持久形象。
content.en_text: I'll is native marsupial is cute, unusual, anil with the baby kangaroo, known as a'joey', safely in its mother's pouch, it presents an enduring image often seen on Australian postcards and posters.

content.cn_text: 然而，这些动物大量繁殖。
content.en_text: Yet these animals breed prolifically.

content.cn_text: 在过去，森林和灌木林被广泛砍伐，为传统的羊和牛产业提供了充足的养料。
content.en_text: and have been highly favoured by the widespread clearing of forest and bushland in the past to provide hind for the traditional sheep and cattle industry.

content.cn_text: 具有讽刺意味的是。
content.en_text: Ironically.

content.cn_text: 袋鼠的繁殖数量如此之多，一般每年可达5000万只。
content.en_text: kangaroos breed in such numbers, up to 50 million per year .generally.

content.cn_text: 他们变成了害虫。
content.en_text: that they have become pests.

content.cn_text: 因此，很长一段时间以来，每年都有一次“淘汰”。
content.en_text: Consequently, for a long lime now, there has been a yearly 'cull'.

content.cn_text: 换句话说，通过控制和精确的射击，他们的数量减少了，这里我们说的是射杀数百万人。
content.en_text: In other words, their numbers are reduced with controlled and accurate shooting and here, we are talking of shooting millions of them.

content.cn_text: 在过去，它们的尸体只是被丢弃在灌木丛中，后来，一些肉被用作猫粮，事实上，现在仍然是这样。
content.en_text: In the past, the carcasses were simply left out in the bush, then Inter, some of the meat was used as cat food, and still is, in fact.

content.cn_text: 直到最近几年，这种食物才被用于人类消费，主要用于海外市场，人们可以理解为什么。
content.en_text: It is only in recent years that the meal has been used for human consumption, primarily for the overseas market, and one can see why.

content.cn_text: 肉是。
content.en_text: The meat is.

content.cn_text: 大家都说，比牛肉更瘦更嫩。
content.en_text: by all accounts, leaner and tenderer than beef.

content.cn_text: 它味道很重，很容易剁碎，但它也有一些更微妙的优点，这是我现在想探讨的话题。
content.en_text: It has a strong taste, and minces easily, but it also has some more subtle advantages, and this is a topic I'd like to explore now.

content.cn_text: 现在，袋鼠肉的市场规模虽小，但仍在增长。
content.en_text: Now, there is a market small but growing for kangaroo meal.

content.cn_text: 鼓励消费的一个有趣的原因是，这样做是环保的。
content.en_text: And one interesting reason to encourage its consumption is that it is environmentally friendly to do so.

content.cn_text: 这本身有三个原因。
content.en_text: and this itself is for three reasons.

content.cn_text: 一个。
content.en_text: One.

content.cn_text: 传统的牛羊大多分布在澳大利亚农村的牧场上，它们是外来物种，对环境有一定的破坏性。
content.en_text: the traditional cattle and sheep which mostly fill the rural pastures of Australia are introduced species, and somewhat destructive to the environment.

content.cn_text: 改变所有。
content.en_text: Alter all.

content.cn_text: 他们本来就不应该出现在那里。
content.en_text: they weren't meant to be there in the first place.

content.cn_text: 它们的饮食习惯破坏了草的根系。
content.en_text: Their eating habits destroy the root systems of grass.

content.cn_text: 它们坚硬的蹄子撕开地面，它们的粪便很重，窒息了新的生长。
content.en_text: their hard hooves tear open the ground, and their droppings are heavy and smothering to new growth.

content.cn_text: 相比之下，袋鼠则完全相反。
content.en_text: Kangaroos, in contrast, have all the opposite traits.

content.cn_text: 它们是一种本地动物，与那里的自然环境和谐共存，所以用袋鼠代替牛是很有意义的。
content.en_text: They are a native animal, existing in harmony with the natural environment there, so replacing cattle with kangaroos makes good sense.

content.cn_text: 另一个好处是，与牛羊不同，袋鼠在消化过程中不会产生甲烷，而甲烷和二氧化碳是导致全球变暖的主要因素之一。最后，由于袋鼠太多了，为了吃肉而捕杀它们的数量增加了澳大利亚野生动物的生物多样性。
content.en_text: The other benefit is that, unlike cows and sheep, kangaroos don't produce methane as a by-product of their digestion and methane, along with carbon dioxide, is one of the primary agents of global warming, finally, since there are too many kangaroos anyway,culling their population for their meat increases the biodiversity of Australia's wildlife.

content.cn_text: 换句话说，小袋鼠，短尾矮袋鼠。
content.en_text: In other words, wallabies, quokkas.

content.cn_text: 袋熊和其他本地动物
content.en_text: wombats, and other native animals are given

content.cn_text: 空间也增加了他们的数量，就像在丛林被广泛清除之前那样。
content.en_text: space to increase their numbers also, in the way that it was meant to he before the bushland was extensively cleared.

content.cn_text: 因此，有人会认为这样的论点使这个案例无可辩驳;然而，还有一些严重的问题需要克服。
content.en_text: So, one would think such arguments make the case irrefutable; however, there are some serious issues to overcome.

content.cn_text: 最大的问题是吃这种肉的方式
content.en_text: The biggest problem is the way eating this meat is

content.cn_text: 感知。
content.en_text: perceived.

content.cn_text: 许多人仍然对吃这种实际上是国家的象征，而且是可爱的象征感到不安。
content.en_text: Many people still feel uneasy about eating what is, in effect, a national symbol, and a cute one at that.

content.cn_text: 除了这个感知问题，袋鼠不能像传统的牛一样被驯化或饲养在围栏里。
content.en_text: As well as this perception problem, kangaroos cannot be domesticated or raised within enclosures, as can conventional cattle.

content.cn_text: 袋鼠只是跳过栅栏，这意味着被捕获的是野生袋鼠，这并不总是有效或划算的。
content.en_text: Kangaroos simply leap over fences, meaning that it is wild kangaroos that are harvested, which is not always efficient or cost effective.

content.cn_text: 这个过程涉及到卡车、射手、聚光灯和许多人来协助从偏远地区运送尸体。
content.en_text: This process involves trucks, shooters, spotlights, and many people to assist in transporting carcasses from remote locations.

content.cn_text: 最后，公众并不喜欢用枪来捕杀这些动物，认为这是不人道的。
content.en_text: Finally, the public are not that happy with guns being the tool to cull these animals, arguing that it is inhumane.

content.cn_text: 当然，专业射手是经过训练的，有执照的，并遵循严格的行为准则。
content.en_text: Professional shooters are, of course, trained, licensed, and follow a strict code of practice.

content.cn_text: 他们使用精确的武器，总是瞄准头部。
content.en_text: They use accurate weapons, and always aim for the head.

content.cn_text: 理论上，死亡是瞬间发生的，但并非所有情况都是如此。
content.en_text: The kill, in theory, is instantaneous, yet not necessarily in all cases.

content.cn_text: 因此，动物权利活动家拍摄的袋鼠拍摄视频可能会给这个行业带来很大的问题。
content.en_text: Thus, videos of kangaroo shooting taken by animal rights' activists can cause great problems for the industry.

content.cn_text: 然而，公众需要意识到的是，最终，他们所有的肉都需要杀死动物，而在牛屠宰场发生的事情，本质上与在普通袋鼠宰杀过程中发生的事情没有什么不同。
content.en_text: Yet what the public needs to realise is that, ultimately, all of their meat requires the killing of animals,and what happens in cattle abattoirs is, in essence, no different to what happens during the average kangaroo cull.

groups.desc:
Questions 31-33
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: Kangaroos
items.analysis: 文中明确指出袋鼠每年繁殖数量高达5000万只，数量如此之多以至于成为了害虫。这直接对应了选项C "exist in large numbers"（大量存在）。文章强调了袋鼠数量之多，需要进行控制，这是袋鼠的主要特征之一，因此C是正确答案。

items.title: 'Culls'
items.analysis: 文中多次提到"shooting"和"shooters"，明确说明了捕杀过程中使用枪支。特别是在描述专业射手时，提到他们"use accurate weapons"（使用精确的武器），这里的"weapons"显然指的就是枪支。此外，文中还提到他们"always aim for the head"（总是瞄准头部），进一步确认了枪支的使用。因此，选项A "involve rifles"（涉及步枪）是正确的。

items.title: Kangaroo meat is
items.analysis: 文中明确指出袋鼠肉曾被用作猫粮，并且这种用途一直持续到现在。这与选项C "sometimes given to pets"完全吻合。"still is"表明这种用途仍在继续，符合"sometimes"的描述。因此，C选项是正确答案。

groups.desc:
Questions 34-40
Complete the notes.
Write ONE WORD ONLY O for each answer.

items.title: Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
items.analysis: 文中提到“environmentally friendly”是袋鼠肉的一个优点，并且解释了为什么袋鼠对环境有利。这正是题干中“good for”所对应的内容。因此，“environment”是正确答案。
文中提到“Kangaroos, in contrast, have all the opposite traits. They are a native animal, existing in harmony with the natural environment there...”，说明袋鼠与澳大利亚的自然环境和谐共存。因此，答案是“harmony”。
文中提到“unlike cows and sheep, kangaroos don't produce methane”，说明袋鼠不会像牛和羊那样产生甲烷。题目中提到“lower amounts of”，即减少某种物质的含量，结合原文可以确定答案是“methane”。因此，正确答案是“methane”。
文中提到，捕杀袋鼠以获取肉类可以增加澳大利亚野生动物的生物多样性，具体来说是给其他本土动物提供了“空间”来增加它们的数量。因此，题干中的“allows...for other animals”对应的正确答案是“space”。
文中提到“the way eating this meat is perceived”，即吃这种肉的方式被认为如何，这与题干“Eating it is ... as bad”相对应。因此，正确答案是“perceived”。文中具体解释了很多人对吃袋鼠肉感到不安，这也印证了“perceived”作为答案的合理性。
因为题干中提到“use of 39”，而原文中提到“guns being the tool to cull these animals”，这里的“guns”就是题干中“use of”所指的工具。因此，答案是“guns”。
因为题干中提到的问题变得更严重的原因是“worse”，而原文中提到“videos of kangaroo shooting taken by animal rights' activists can cause great problems for the industry.”，说明这些视频导致了问题的加剧。因此，答案是“videos”。
